title: Runbook: Manifest Fetch Timeouts
bodyMarkdown:
How to respond when a restore fails during the manifest fetch stage rather than stalling silently.

## Symptoms
The restore fails fast rather than hanging, with an error naming the manifest path and the configured deadline. This is a different failure mode than the stall covered in the checkpoint restore runbook, that runbook is for restores that hang with no error at all.

## Diagnosis
The fetch in src/restore/fetcher.ts is bounded by HELIOS_MANIFEST_TIMEOUT_MS, thirty seconds by default. When object storage is slow to respond, usually under load or during a regional degradation, the deadline trips before the body finishes downloading and the fetch aborts with a clearly named error rather than hanging indefinitely.

## Fix
Check object storage status first, this is often a symptom of a broader storage slowdown rather than anything specific to Helios. If storage is healthy but a particular manifest is unusually large, raising HELIOS_MANIFEST_TIMEOUT_MS for that restore is reasonable. Retries already happen automatically through the backoff wrapper around the fetch.

## Escalation
If fetches are timing out broadly across restores, page the storage on-call and check with the object storage provider directly. Priya Natarajan owns this runbook.
